star_1.text: The single best game. Nostalgic as F. Love it. The original game mid-summer 2017 was absolutely astonishing to me. Limited ads and many spaceships to play through the game. This was the first game I didn't mind paying to get rid of ads and even after that I continued to support it by watching ads to try out ships, or continue on in the infinite mode and etc. I loved the fact that they were among the few who did not shove WATCH ADS down our throats. Although now it became just that. Plz revert.

7/16/20 Edit: The latest updates locked all my previously unlocked ships, and raised prices on items that could be previously obtained through ads. Also removed the ability gain crystals through ads daily. Not interested in playing this anymore... Fun game, good music. Seems to be an issue with the "free" crystals button. Sometimes, it straight up doesn't work. Other times, you've got to click it a bunch of times to find the right spot for it to take you to the ad

Update: After playing this, I can no longer recommend this game. The devs have gotten very greedy and has so many ads it is now no longer fun. Before 1 ad would give you 3+ games. Now have to watch 2 ads min per game. Screw that. Update 1: So there seems to be a glitch in their "new" rewards system. I had 19 keys because I turned in all rewards and after playing one game, went back and it defaulted to 1. Lost 18 keys. 5 Star: Great retro game and so far the ads are very well balanced.

This game is pretty good. good graphics and whetever else. I'm giving it one star because I purchases the "No Ads" deal for $4.99 or whatever. Now I decide to go on a longterm playing spree and I notice that there a limit to how many games one can play in a certain time span. This is absolutely unacceptable. And the only reasin I am not deleting this game at this moment is because i spent money on it. But next time this becomes a hindernis i will delete this game.

Great and fun game until they added full screen ads that you have to watch unless you pay $5 to go ad free. Before ads were optional to get an extra life or power ups. The grind gets pretty intense once you have to start spending 10k crystals per incremental upgrade. On another note, I'd probably spend $10 to buy the game (ad free) if the progression was better.

,,game system improvements " in one year you only changed colors from red to purple..the price with x5 pieces from 10000->12000 ..You did nothing new in game..no ships , a second and free drone(left) without spend in game, no more stages or less hard stages..with the Primula , I cannot pass stage 10 and I have all to maxed. You don't even care to build better this game. There are no bugs in game, you just updated to appear in eyes of new players, this game is not dead..how all see that.

Can no longer save my progress to google. Old: Sound issues fixed, thank you. I still find it strange for enemies to appear from behind me in infinite mode. How is that fair? I have no way of shooting them, let alone seeing them with my thumb blocking the bottom right corner, and it is even worse when they are off screen to the side because when I quickly move to the other side I just ram into them. I've died within seconds due to the enemies coming from the bottom. I find the to be poor game design. Another issue is when destroying a large enemy, the explosions makes your movement lag.

This game used to be so much fun back in 2016/17 when it launched. Free coin multiplier in higher difficulties, 5 free trial rounds with each unlocked ship, reasonable upgrade prices... now its full of ads, literally after every single round ot gives you an unskippable ad, wings buffs are random and tied to ads now too, ship trial is ad-bound, adding of lootboxes, no more free coin multiplier in high difficulties, while no improvements to the actual gameplay whatsoever. Devs got greedy.

I don't know when but the "improvement" on the abilities wiped out all my previous upgrades. Had a decent damage to face the enemies on Hero difficult and now everything is gone and have to go all way back to Novice again, not fair. Also I cannot upgrade abilities as I want because is a random thing now, really? Loved the gameplay, art and music but now I feel disappointed.
Watch an add at the end of a round to get a bonus. Opt out of it and you are still forced to watch an ad.

If weren't for the bug that I'm getting this would be the best game that I played so far, don't get me wrong I love this game but sometimes I can't shoot, pause, press something or move at all and the enemies shoot like a hundred times but their bullets and missiles stay in place, then when someone crash on me I die and start shooting but can't move either, but I realized that I'm able now to pause the game and when I resume the game I can move and shoot but my bullets instantly kill them

It's okay, its art is pretty cool, but like every other sky fighting game it's pay to win, there's the loot boxes everyone hates, there's the "premium" planes, the power ups, and whatever else they have that I didn't want to see. I wish I could say it was worth the install, but sadly no

Coming back to this game after a while I see my progress and all my space ships are completely missing even though I saved my game through google play even my purchases are gone , I would grind again but seeing as everything has increased in price I dont see the point. This is a really good game though......

Great design, and that's all. What they call "free play" on the only "play button" is actually to see an ad and get only 3 free plays, and that doesn't count every other ad when you finish a battle. The game itself seems repetitive, weapons are boring.

Used to be good and fun game to kill some time. But after latest update all of the features that used to be obtainable via watching ads has become expensive purchaseable features. The ads I could live with but I'm not going to spend 6 euros minimum for some nifty features to make it easier to play.

Used to be a great little game with a small, accaptable amount of ads. Now it is so littered with ads you basically spend 80% of your time waiting for ads to complete to even play the game. While the game itself runs smoothly and controls feel good, the ads ruin the entire experience and fun of the game. It feels like a spamware infected game.

Erased all purchases with an update. Increased prices and increased ads, as well as making the game harder to encourage pay to win. This WAS a great game and i gave it 5 stars but the devs decided to be greedy, in more than one way, and stole everything i bought so they can charge more for it. Gross business practices.

The developer for this game is dishonest. Don't purchase the ad removal because it doesnt work. I purchased it and after noticing that it didnt remove any of the ads, I contacted the developer and they convienently waited 48 hours after my purchase to tell me that they would not refund the fee and I could take my problem to Google play if I didn't like their decision. Google play won't refund a purchase fee after 48 hours. So screw this developer. App Uninstalled.

Game freezes at the end of leveling up the first ship and doesn't allow to continue playing into the second world which is a fustration to figure out its just a bugged out game 5 minutes later, had fun the firdt level and lost the joy right afterwards, disappointed with the lack of freedom in the game.

This used to be the best game but it has become ruined by greed and pay to play. I do not recommend this game as of august 1st 2020 This is the best scroll shooter on the app store. Great graphics, great variety with the different ships, and great menu. I love scroll shooters, and I love pixel graphics, and this game satisfies both.

star_2.text: This game used to be great. At one point, you were able to hustle enough coins to purchase ships. Now, in an attempt to get more money, you can only use coins to purchase lower tier ships. Also, the elf wings you could get every ten minutes. Instead of that now you have to watch an ad. Not only that, you typically only get a Shield boost or magnetic field increase. Lame.

This game is a super fun idea and i enjoyed playing it. You also get fairly rewarded to watch advertisments. One major issue I found kind of ridiculous. In order to get a three star on the further levels you have to watch advertisements no matter if you performed a lot better on that level. It feels like EA's Pay to win and i don't recommend that on any one who likes earning stuff without having to watch advertisments or have to spend 3 bucks on something so little.

(To: Developers) In short, all these new features and systems are fun and really good but I just can't even imagine what went through your mind when you buffed the boss HP. It doesn't even matter if you upgrade the main weapon... It takes 5 minutes to take it down, and after 2-3 minutes the battle area fills with enemy shots and there is no way to dodge. It is just boring and stupid. And since the amount of reward is the same, the game feels even less rewarding...

Good little game, despite the retro feel, but it does take me back to the snes and sega days. 1st issue is that on normal mode the game seems to keep sticking now and then, which can be annoying, and on infinite mode the game sticks alot more. And sometimes is unplayable and keep having to start over, multiple times. Other than than, I do like it a lot. I also like how the music and backgrounds change as well as you've even put weather and lightning effects. That makes the game more appealing.

It was ok at first but then the grind set in and the only way to improve was to grind for better ships. The worst of it is that after the latest update I could get anything to work or play any levels and so I reinstalled the app and found that I lost some progress. I was set back by losing some items, coins and two ships I recently got by grinding to unlock. So not the greatest experience but it was fun to a certain point.

Fun game but don't waste your money. This game is pretty fun but don't spend the money to get rid of the ads. It only gets rid of the small ad at the bottom of the screen on the level selection screen. You don't get an unlimited number of plays which is totally unreasonable for spending $2.99. You can get 3 more plays by... you guessed it, watching an ad. Take some advice and let people play as many times as they want for spending $2.99 on your game. Don't be that greedy.

The game in itself is amazing. I was happy to pay to remove the ads in order to show some support. There's a clear difference between smart business plan and dishonesty, however. More than 70$ to buy all the last ships? Without any way to buy them with crystals? Are you guys out of your mind? I was hoping that could be unlocked if I reached a certain level but it doesn't seem to be the case. I'm always willing to show support but that's just dishonest.

Optional ads for rewards and the game won't let you forget that. Ads for everything. Several gacha mechanics too. I hated the game most for not allowing the player to turn off vibrations until after the first level and a bit of hand holding tutorial. That was a terrible game design.

First of all, this game crashed so hard I had to reboot my phone the very first time I played it. Not a good start! Aside from that? Typical mobile shmup. Decent retro-style voxel graphics, pleasant soundtrack, though ill-fitting at times, not much in the way of interesting play/score mechanics, WAY too grindy. Takes forever to buy upgrades at level 4 and all enemies become massive bullet sponges. No thanks.

Way too many Ads, even after you pay for it. Want to try a new ship? need a buff for that awfully OP boss? need to double the measly reward points for beating that level? Watch a 30 second ad. And the level designs are repetitive. This game is purely a cash grab.

Too loud, no sound controls. Unable to be used while listening to a podcast or something. Improve that and the rating would definitely go up 2 more stars. Still seems too microtransaction ladden, but after 30 minutes of play it's too early to tell

Boldly and explicitly made ptw with reduced max stats and literal gacha mechanics with the last update. Increased difficulty is appreciated, enemy bullet-sponginess is not. It's a shame there was a middle ground/compromise here to increase revenue and devs overshot it by a mile. Not going to be picking this up again :/

The game was addicting, up until the point that it was unrealistically hard to continue. Also, the ads got worse and worse. I feel like this game is designed for maximum ad revenue, bait and switch. I wouldn't bother with this game.

Woah, they completely overhaul the game. I lost a lot of upgrades I purchased because they changed a lot of things. Planes are also a lot more expensive than before. They took away a lot of things I liked about the previous version. :(

Hey! You like games that double your rewards after watching an ad? You like games that let you click the home button after watching an ad? Hey! You like games that make you watch an ad EVERY TIME YOU CLICK A BUTTON? Well, if so, then this is the game for you!

Wow the new update really ruin the game. Everything now is pay to win. Even grinding and watching the ads wont do you much. The game will constantly try to make you spend money every playthrough, best to play offline. Have fun grinding.

Good game, but impossible to pass lvl 5 without using money or tons of ads for doubling the money. Mobs to strong to kill with 'normal gold drop rate' so you either pay or grind low lvls till you want to die.
Could have been fun but hampered by too many ads and being 30 seconds long. Ads should be 5 seconds max. Game pacing is also too slow. Levels are too short
Looks like a fun game but as soon as I opened the app the game would never load. It just stays on the black screen of death until you forcefully close out the app.
After update 3.0.9 not all enemies destroyed give coins... When you reach higher levels make you want to quit the game. Just uninstalled.

star_3.text: Fun shooter. I miss this game. I'm glad it's still around! I lowered my score! They increased the amount of keys needed to get the best planes just as I was getting close to winding down and completing my collection. Instead of adding another plane they took the lesser road and added the option to watch more ads and a need to watch more ads. Saddened but it's a good game and if you spend a little money you will have a good time.

Great game. Challenging at times, so it keeps me engaged. I havent played it in a few months, and i just got back to playing it today... And it seems to be harder when you get to bosses. They are bosses after all! Although I did prefer the pause button to be the 2nd finger tap. EDIT: On the S23 Ultra and having issues. The ship controls are very shaky and jumpy. Lost pinpoint accuracy and unfortunately makes it borderline unplayable.

Really fun retro game. Good music, backdrops and most importantly fun gameplay. Unfortunately, by the time you get to stage 9 you will be forced to either make in app purchases to upgrade your ship or watch a ton of advertisements to double in game currency from missions as the upgrades get significantly more expensive while the rewards increase in much smaller increments.

Excellent game, but would it please be possible to collect all achievements in one go (with a press of a button)? At the moment, it takes me 20 minutes of pressing the collect coins button in order to collect all achievements I've unlocked, only because it goes level by level (for example for every 50k coins collected, when you collected millions I let you imagine how long it takes to press the collect button...). Really ridiculous issue. Thanks.

I updated when it was switched to the key system for unlocking ships. Really kind of took away from the gameplay. I've been stuck on a few levels and have been just making cash and watching ads for months. Have not earned a class b it above ship yet. Tried the upgrade route, spent lots of time gathering coin and upgrading to make precisely zero difference. It is just a slog in the mud, wallowing in your own impotence. Slack up a little, find that balance and I'll gladly go back to 5 starts again

Great game with terrible micro transactions problem. It's just a terrible way to make a f2p game. Expensive upgrades with very very little reward you'll get by completing levels. It's impossible to enjoy the game without spending at least a good amount of money. Later stages just gonna get worse and worse just bcuz you are not paying. Was planning to 100% the game but will delete it as soon as the campaign ends. The Dev team just doesn't care for players. It's only the money they only care about

Overall great play and graphics. My main complaint is that there seems to be a glitch or bug with some ships I was supposed to get for free by achievements, however these have been perpetually locked with a "50% off" tag. Feels like its made to get you to purchase the thing even tho you're CLEARLY not supposed to.

This games is fun to play, but something needs a tweak in the balancing department. However it is a freemium game. U have to either watch ads after all ur lives are finished or pay for more lives. It doesn't matter if u complete successfully, a life was used. Be careful for those ppl who think u buying unlimited lives becuz that isn't an option. Overall it's a fun game, but devs concern is cashing in on an experience then to just making a paid version without all the scummy practices.

Few game design poor decisions. When player is at the edge of screen he can't see what's happening on another part of battlefield. It gives us inability to avoid some bullets and we can easily miss an enemy but killing everyone is vital. Lava level is just terrible, can't see projectiles and last boss is shooting from behind player's ship. Hitboxes of the ships aren't clear, this leads to huge problems with ""bullet hell"" when it sometimes happens. On bosses especially.
playing ads for extra items and rewards doesn't work.

Graphs, FX are nice. But the width of the game that much bigger than the screen is a gameplay killer. If the background was moving but not the ennemies/bullets/coins when we go left/right it'd be fine. But here after less than 2 runs you feel nauseous already. Sorry for the guy responsible for that but damn ! the sounds are truly terrible. Midi sounds in PC games 30 years ago were better. I played Hawk Freedom for 2 years. Retro Shooting (nothing "retro" in there btw) for 2 minutes. Go figure.

The game is great, but is so...repetitive maybe? I played 15 stages and the scenarios are the same, the enemies are the same, and the challenges of stage are the same (don't use skill, use skill 6 times, kill all the enemies, don't get shoot). Of you like classic shooters like Darius, this game is not for you.

a great game so far, but the stage mode is waaaay too easy, and the infinite mode is waaaay too hard, so I can't find a good balance of challange within the game. Im not sure if i have upgrade my ship to the max to enjoy inifinite mode, or if it's just made to be that hard. Not hard because there are too much attacks coming at me, but because my bullets barely do any damage, even to the low level yellow ships thing

Mostly game is fun to play, is visually great but amount of ads is frustrating. After the level you are given a choice to go to the title screen or to watch some ads but whatever you press you HAVE to watch an advertisement. BTW When you go back to the game from pause menu time scale is making it look like the game is stuttering, but it doesn't at the moment.

This is a fun game, but there are some problems. Even though I own all the planes required for a collection bonus (increase shooting speed +5%, increase shield time +20%) it is still locked for me. The weekly boss challenge screens are not in English. Great that you can convert extra keys into coins, but, as others say, we need to be able to unlock *all* drones too.

This game needs a lot of work! Its hard to get power ups and green shards unless you have magnetic power up. Its like you have to have the ship at dead center to pick up anything. And its hard to see the difference between enemy explosion debris, player gun projectiles, and power ups/green shards. The retro look sucks! You can do better than that even for retro! Its at Nintendo level retro graphics. And this game is clearly a Skyforce ripoff! (Skyforce 5 stars btw)

This game is very boring. I played some other shmups like Touhou Project or Cave, and this game is really easy compared to these. Bullet patterns are poor, even bosses can't kick your ass. Graphics are... okay, at least they don't make you cry. Only good thing about it is music. Music is pretty cool, I would say that it's awesome. Overall, this game doesn't worth your time. It sucks.

Good game. However, there has long been a bug in this game where the special actions revert back to the Left side of the screen once the level is complete/over. It does become very frustrating, I have to switch the special actions to the right side of the screen for every mission. The other options are saved, just never switching to R side special actions. I play on a Samsung s20 5g. Developers please fix this issue so I can enjoy this game again.

Really fun up until stage 8, then it becomes way too hard, and not in a good way. Ennemies simply become huge health bags and you will have to go back and farm currency to be able to down them before they leave the screen. Other than that it's a nice way to kill time in the metro or on the toilet

The stylus in the controls are jittery specially when doing slow micro movements, now at first I thought it was my phone but after playing other games and looking at the slider of the controls settings i can tell it's the game itself, like every time I swipe slowly the plane just tilts then teleports a few meters twords the direction I was dragging

star_4.text: The game is actually pretty good in general, boasting tight control and great performance. The micro transactions are fair, allowing you to get all the ships and drones for about $70ish, with each premium ship also granting an additional benefit, like doubling end of mission rewards without ads and some such. The main complaints I have are there are no seasonal events and rewards, and every time you start a mission, you hear an annoying child voice yell, "Start!" Even if they couldn't add seasonal levels and stuff like that, it would be great if they removed that stupid voice. Music and sound great otherwise.

It's nice! It's a 3D pixel plane fighter game. I almost want to call it a bullet hell game, but it's slightly tamer than one of those. Picking up individual coins is kinda hard since you actually have to touch them. And speaking of coins, you're gonna need a lot of them to get by when it comes to the game. There are A LOT of ships in here to try and unlock so you won't be bored. Sit a spell and try it!

A surprisingly good game; a hidden gem 💎 ! But I'm only giving it 4 stars at this point, because it doesn't seem to be compatible with all devices... 😒 I have this downloaded on my HTC (Android) but couldn't download it onto my Samsung tablet - also an Android device - for some reason. But nevertheless I'm hooked; crisp graphics, cool BGM, and smooth gameplay! Definitely a great, intense shoot-'em-up! 🚀👍

Good and very responsive control. Art style is nice, love the planes voxels. The only draw back is the color code for enemy projectiles can sometimes be confusing. Maybe they should be more into red and orange while collectibles green/blue it will make it more recognizable in dense battles (the tanks projs are almost same shade of blue as blue diamonds only brighter).

Great game that needs controller support to shine. Touch screen controls are inadequate beyond the rookie difficulty, your finger obstructs the view of the ship and the minefield of projectiles you're dodging. My finger tips also get sweaty in short order and make smooth, precise movement very difficult. I suppose I could buy a stylus, but that is still worse than a controller with dpad. It's frustrating because this is truly a great top down shooter.

Solid 4/5 experience. Would recommend this game to anyone looking for a classic topdown shooter. Biggest complaint: you can't navigate anywhere in the app without it suggesting you buy something. Plus the ads have grown more persistent (one even requires me to press the x to close the task, ratherthan being able to hit the back button like a normal person.

Great shooter with an original twist where the playing field is a little wider than the screen making you move the field of view as you pan horizontally. The panning took me a few levels to get used to but, despite not having had any difficulties with it after the first hour of playtime, I still find myself getting a bit annoyed at not being able to see the full screen like in other games of this genre. Still enjoying the game though, so have to give props to the devs for trying new things.

This is a really fun game for time spent idling but I am really confused as to why the yellow enemy missiles and the yellow coins are the same colour! Visual clarity is incredibly important in a bullet-hell game, which is why this is so bizarre to me. I also want to point out that I'm not colourblind.

It's an enjoyable, surprisingly addictive, shooter in a sea of similar games and it seperates itself. Gameplay is solid and the art style is fun. My only gripe is how the majority of the good stuff is stuck behind a paywall, but that doesn't mean you can't have fun with this game. It just means you might not enjoy it to its fullest and that's kind of a shame. I get it, it's free, but I'd rather deal with a few more ads than being forced to spend money on stuff that should be unlockable.

This seems like a decent blaster...however, I find the presentation totally confusing. I'm guided to click on items without knowing what will happen. A guide would be very helpful. That said, it's a good game with lots of power ups to earn. Ads not intrusive. Well worth investigating...thank you, developers.

Pleasing 8-bit aesthetic with style to spare, good control, and extremely balanced difficulty. Just hard enough to keep me coming back for more! Micro-payments do not cause issues: just replay until you can afford power-ups to make it deeper into the game. Developer has chosen a fitting moniker: "Shmup Holic," indeed.

A great game to kill a few minutes or a few hours. Has an excellent difficulty curve that is easy enough in the beginning but becomes insane later on, the infinity mode is great. The in app purchases at 100% optional and not need to advance at all.

No issues with controls or gameplay, but the best ships are behind some pretty serious paywalls. I like the slower pace compared to other shooters like it, and the graphics are a nice. Could use a bit of polishing and maybe another mode, perhaps pvp?

It's one of the best arcade games on my phone! However, after signing into Google Play games, I had to complete the tutorial level again to unlock the achievement for completing the tutorial, which I did. Are there bugs to fix for other players?

This game is one of my favorite shoot'em up since the Raiden series. There is only one issue. I start playing, and the backgrounds the stage previews won't appear. What appears while playing is the bg the infinite game mode displays. Please fix!!

It's really good fun! The graphics are a cool blend of nostalgic, but, also slick. The levels start easy and I enjoy the way you are able to upgrade your ship. Not many ads, but, they are well placed. Great game, highly recommend.

It's pretty indepth, but some things aren't explained or misleading. The UI (even after the update) is still unclear in areas. Overall, the game is fun and requires some thought and planning, which is good.

I love the look and the gameplay of the game. But I still got some suggestions. I would let the enemies bullets more shiny, to make it easier to decide between the own ones and the bullets from the enemies.

This game has gone ways to define and distinguish itself from a well counterpart of the same game genre, aswell as give useful in-game upgrade feature of weapons. Honestly I'd like to give it a 4 for how the mission feature is designed aswell as other added upgrades. Aswell as the bonuses to be made before missions, it makes this game highly addictive.

It's a decent space shooter game. It's very strait forward and easy to operate. But I would like to see customization of ships for example custom skins to suit a user's preference. But keep improving the game.

star_5.text: Best shoot em up. Best game i have found. Challenging to the max. So many levels. So many ships. (Loved the free trials). You handle ads as well or better than most developers. Keep on trucking. To add, i have never watched so many ads. It is great. You all dont make it difficult to watch the ad theres lots of true 5 second ads. And the rewards for watching are worth it. Great job

I really like the gameplay & graphics, which gives this game an 80's vibe to it! The controls seem a bit stiff (at first) but you'll get the hang of rolling side to side as the game progresses. My only dislike are repetitive the Ads. While Sky Wings may appear as a "simple", easy-to-play retro game, each level gets more intense aside from the opponents becoming aggressive. But, hey don't let that discourage you! I'd like to see a sequel to Sky Wings in future!

Very fun space shooter. I love the asthetic! It's retro in a minecraft-y way and is pleasing to the eye. I've been playing for an hour now with the starter ship and have not been bothered by ads at all. so far, unlocking other ships depends on competed missions which have been easily done with the initial ship. This is a "fly through the stage killing enemies" not a Galaga-style "enemies file in and attack" game. Hopefully that makes sense. Highly recommended game.

NOW THIS IS AN ARCADE SHOOTER! cannot tell you how many games these days are just a grab for cash. The action is well paced, the prices for things are fair many with options to view an ad to obtain. Thank you to the company for making a game that is very playable and I gotta say I love the voxel graphics! You guys built beautiful levels with bomb gameplay...no pun intended!

Very fun with lots of replayability. A cool shooter with lots of options. At first I thought there was a lot of ads but there really isn't. When you play as you get a bonus like two times coins or to try out a ship instead of buying it. So there can be a lot of ads but you actually get something every time you watch one which is pretty cool too. And Those ads are instead of paying for an upgrade so you can pay for stuff instead of watching the ads or you can just skip the bonuses all together and you don't have to watch any.

I downloaded this about a week ago and I'm loving how this game makes use of ads. Instead of having them pop up at random you can watch them when you want for decent in game rewards. On to the game itself to keep it brief it's a vertical arcade shooter with visuals that makes good use of the blocky pixels that make up the ships and levels and it has a nice chill soundtrack. Most importantly it's a fun way to kill time.

Update: still playing in '22, 5years later. I keep coming back, and it keeps evolving. There's more in-app purchases now... But still a great game. One of the top 10 phone games I've played. Stick with it - without buying anything, I'm still enjoying it! Finally got to world 8, exploring all the new ships. Devs are clever, very active, very responsive. I want to buy something just to support them. Great game, great controls, great dynamics and visuals, and EVERYTHING BLOWS UP!!

This game is awesome! I usually get bored of mobile games quickly but I keep coming back to play this one, it's kept my attention for a while now. Graphics are great as is the game play. The dev's implementation of ads in this game should be the new standard of how ads are incorporated in apps. The ad implementation is so good that I don't even mind the small number of ads that show up. The ONLY thing I'd change is to have an option to pause the game when you remove your finger from the screen.

I've played all kinds of games. Mobile games alone? At least one thousand, so far; and as much as I enjoy a decent bullet hell type game, I've always been frustrated with the severe lack of good ones to choose from. I'm glad to have tried this game, it's certainly worth a download, and I'm happy to play it. It's fun, about the right amount of challenging, though more upgrades are always welcome, and I'd truly love to see huge skill trees involved, as it currently is, I definitely recommend it.

This is a really great game to play when you're bored. The gameplay is interesting and dynamic, while in-app purchases are completely optional and not necessary to have fun. There's a lot of grinding involved to upgrade and unlock new ships (unless you pay) but at least for me this is perfectly fine since it's just a mobile game, and the gameplay itself is fun even if you are just using the starter ship. I do have to mention that getting the higher S-class ships is essentially impossible without spending money, but again, having a rare ship doesn't really make the game any more fun. The bosses are also very skill based and unique, which makes it an even more fun experience. Although there are ads, you can play without wifi and it works perfectly fine. I play this game whenever I am bored or have nothing to do.

Fun and easy to play. And while there are ships that you can buy exclusively by money, its not so much a cash grab as there are a decent selection of ships you can buy with in-game coins. It doesn't feel like pay-to-win like some other games out there. Long story short: played it, loved it, highly recommend!

I've only played for a day and I already love it! It's a near-endless amount of fun! It's so hard these days to find a mobile game that doesn't shoot an ad at your face after every stage, and there's one that doesn't. Frankly, I'm considering to buy something in-game to support the devs, maybe one of the A or S rank ships (that you can only get with real money, but to be fair you get a LOT for free)

This is a very cool game. Instantly playable, retro feel with '8 bit' graphics and no stupid ads popping up either! In fact you can gain extra coins and bonuses by watching ads when you want, so you're in control of that. It's not a game of frenzy, chaos and explosions. Instead it has the pace of a classic 80s arcade game (I remember them well) so props to the developers for replicating that experience with Sky Wings.

Its a good game, nice graphics, and the way that they made it 3d is extremely awsome, i love the model of the fighters as well, mostly the dandelion, burning sky, and kung fu boy =v=, for the ads, I dont really mind since I only play this game offline, and the last one is that there are still little bugs, like In my entire time of playing, there were 2 that I found, first was somehow my shield didnt go out even tho its on 0% making me op, and second was when my gun somehow stoped, thank you 👍
Seems legit. 1-2 days of playing, plenty of free stuff. Happy days. No network connection needed either. Fun little shoot'em up, with potentially high caps.

I love these type games and especially this one. Maybe because the enemies had thought out into them, there is a lot of planes and upgrades, it is difficult after a few stages, you feel like you need skill not just 10 dollars, etc. Its like sky force in that way I think. Anyway for them what made this thank you it is beautiful and well done.

This game is the single Best  shoot'em up game on the playstore. The sound effects are top notch👌 and so are the variety of enemies and bosses and the music definetly adds a whole lot to the gameplay. The retro style graphics make the game look so good that it deserves a 5 star rating from anyone who plays it. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

To everyone complaining about the adds... Literally just turn off data if it bothers you. I love the way the game handles continues (add after you die from continuing not before) and that it can be played offline with no issues!! The controls are incredibly tight and super quick if you know what you're doing.

The best Galaga app available gotta be agile 1 hit K.Os wait for the right moments to use abilities and you should be fine. Airstrike clears thee screeen of projectiles and deals high damage, shield blocks them and every ship has a unique Laser. Late in the game it gets very difficult because you just can't do enough damage to destroy all of the enemies I can dodge fine but getting Lord Of the Sky is pretty much impossible if you're free to play. Some ships need upgrading or are hard to aim with

This is the best game for (vertical shooter)" Gameplay feel smooth no lag and hardly ads" Graphics is really clean, very good design. The music sound really good, really blend in the gameplay.  I like the control and added sensitivity, on the side give you special for more powerful weapon attack. When you wanna upgrade you have option to choose your ship after you play through each mission grabbing money. Yes I like it and I would recommend this to anyone , whos a 90's vertical gamer!
